rationale: The citing paper is an experimental study of identity congruency in charitable donations, while the cited paper is a formal/lab duopoly pricing model for conspicuous goods (snob vs. conformist demand), indicating a substantial domain and method mismatch. With only a bare DOI match in the reference list and no evidence of direct method/theory adoption in the provided notes, this looks most like a background social-effects citation rather than substantive use.
